I’ve always preferred to appeal to people’s better nature when getting them to follow rules in school - both pupils and staff. <div>
</div><div>This liberal-from-the-outset approach is appreciated by most, though I have learnt that it doesn’t suit every context or individual. Nonetheless, I was pleased to discover that the British Police have summarised the approach that I have always subconsciously taken, making it much more memorable: Engage - Explain - Encourage - Enforce.</div><div><div>
</div><div>Here’s a graphic explaining how the approach works in the context of the current COVID-19 situation. Something similar might work well in staff handbooks and pupil planners:</div></div><div>
</div><div></div>
